element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic rotation and placement in first few pieces
Player adapts to unpredictability; learns that I-pieces are scarce relative to demand for Tetrises
Player discovers Tetris (4-line clear) is worth 1200(n+1) vs single worth 40(n+1); learns value hierarchy
Player experiences first speed increase; learns pacing mechanic
Progressive difficulty; each level noticeably faster than previous
Brief respite before subsequent speed jumps; teaches player to expect variation in difficulty curve
Sparse but significant speed jumps; player must adapt to irregular acceleration pattern
Pieces fall 1 grid cell per frame; game becomes 'kill screen' designed as unplayable ceiling

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available from game start via mode select
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Alternative challenge: clear 25 lines at fixed level with randomized bottom blocks; rewards spatial problem-solving over endurance
Player agency in difficulty; hidden extension (hold A-button) rewards discovery

typical_relief_triggers: Completing a Tetris (4-line clear) - highest score reward
Reaching a level plateau (no speed increase for 2-3 levels)
Clearing lines before field fills to top
Receiving an I-piece after drought period
typical_tension_triggers: Extended drought of I-pieces (necessary for Tetrises)
Randomness denying optimal piece for current field state
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating tension with irregular difficulty spikes. Early game (Levels 0-10) features consistent speed increases every level. Mid-game (Levels 11-28) shows varied pacing with plateaus and sudden jumps. Late game (Level 29+) reaches designed unplayability. Overall arc: predictable introduction → unpredictable acceleration → impossible speed. Score incentive (Tetris worth 30x single clear) creates constant tension between safe play and risky high-reward stacking.

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden level extension: holding A-button during level select adds 10 additional starting levels (0-19 instead of 0-9)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction: Levels 0-2 at slowest speeds; player learns piece mechanics and basic stacking without pressure. Level 0 can be selected to minimize speed concerns.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate acceleration: Levels 3-9 increase speed linearly with each level. Levels 10-12 plateau briefly. Levels 13-19 introduce irregular speed jumps (only at 13, 16, 19). Player must now balance efficiency (Tetris stacking for score) with safety (avoiding topout).
difficulty_curve.late_game: Extreme difficulty: Levels 20-28 maintain high speed with jumps at 22, 25, 28. Level 29+ reaches 'kill screen' where pieces fall 1 cell/frame—designed as unbeatable. Developers never expected play past Level 29 (display corrupts beyond this point). Modern players extend play via novel rolling techniques discovered in 2020s.
notable_spikes: Level 10→13 (jump from consistent progression to irregular spacing)
Level 19→20 (exit last regular jump, enter true endurance zone)
Level 29 (kill screen: fall speed maxes out, game designed to end)

notable_level_patterns.description: Every level increases tetromino fall speed. Player experiences consistent difficulty curve suitable for learning mechanics. Teaches expectation of progression.
Levels 11-12 maintain speed of Level 10. Levels 13, 16, 19, 25, 28 introduce sudden jumps. Intervening levels hold constant. Creates unpredictable tension spikes for skilled players.
Speed maxes out at 1 cell/frame. Game display glitches for levels >29. Designed as hard stop; modern techniques bypass this ceiling. Represents designer's intended game endpoint.

Score formula: Single=40(n+1), Double=100(n+1), Triple=300(n+1), Tetris=1200(n+1) where n=level. Tetris is 30x more valuable than Single at equivalent difficulty. Creates constant risk/reward tension: safe single clears vs. precarious high-line stacks.

Clear 25 lines at fixed player-selected level with randomized obstacle blocks at bottom. No speed increase. Emphasizes spatial problem-solving over endurance. Alternative difficulty curve for players seeking different challenge type.
